behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names both files (download.ts, crBrowser.ts), the cancelDownload(uuid) method, the Download class, CRBrowserContext, Target.disposeBrowserContext, and the public-method naming convention from CLAUDE.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what needs to happen (expose cancel method, cancel downloads before disposal) without providing exact code or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests accept varied patterns (regex for cancel method, any this.<field> reference, any .cancel() call within 50 lines before dispose) rather than requiring specific variable names.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run npm build/tsc/lint subprocesses and use node -e to parse the class structure; though behavioral checks are mostly static analysis, they go beyond simple substring checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_not_stub requires cancelDownload in body, test_cancel_uses_instance_field requires this.<field>, test_context_close_cancels_downloads requires both .cancel() and _downloads reference before disposeBrowserContext.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover both required behaviors: public cancel() method on Download and cancellation before context disposal.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived) and annotated with [static]/[pr_diff]/[agent_config] tags.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code-only, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers match the repo structure (packages/playwright-core/src/server/...).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files to look at (which is a standard 'Files to Look At' section) but does not specify line numbers or exact code changes.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no agentmd config edits.
